Draws and updates an action button on a Pebble smartwatch interface using specified graphics contexts.

# Purpose
The code defines functions for drawing and updating an action button in a graphical user interface. It includes two main functions: [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>) and [`action_button_update_proc`](<#action_button_update_proc>). The [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>) function is responsible for rendering an oval-shaped button on a specified layer using a given fill color. It calculates the button's position and size based on the layer's bounds and the user's preferred content size. The function uses the `grect_align` function to align the button to the right side of the window and offsets it based on predefined size settings.

The [`action_button_update_proc`](<#action_button_update_proc>) function serves as a layer update procedure. It calls [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>) to render the button with a default fill color of `GColorBlack`. The code imports necessary graphics and content size utilities from the `applib` library, indicating that it is part of a larger application framework. The code is intended to be used within a graphical application, likely as part of a user interface component that requires a consistent button appearance across different display sizes and user preferences.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `action_button.h`
- `applib/graphics/graphics.h`
- `applib/preferred_content_size.h`


# Functions

---
### action\_button\_draw<!-- {{#callable:action_button_draw}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_button.c#L22>)

Draws an action button on a layer, aligning it to the right side of the window and offsetting it based on content size preferences.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to the `GContext` used for drawing operations.
    - ``layer``: A pointer to the `Layer` on which the action button is drawn.
    - ``fill_color``: The `GColor` used to fill the action button.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Get the bounds of the `layer` to determine the drawing area.
    - Set the `radius` of the button based on whether the display is round or not.
    - Initialize a `GRect` for the button with a size based on the `radius`.
    - Align the `GRect` to the right side of the `bounds` using [`grect_align`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>).
    - Offset the `GRect` horizontally by the `radius` to position it halfway off-screen.
    - Further offset the `GRect` based on the preferred content size using `PREFERRED_CONTENT_SIZE_SWITCH`.
    - Set the fill color in the graphics context to `fill_color`.
    - Draw an oval in the `GRect` using [`graphics_fill_oval`](<../graphics/graphics_circle.c.md#graphics_fill_oval>) with `GOvalScaleModeFitCircle`.
- **Output**: No return value; the function performs drawing operations on the provided `GContext`.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`grect_align`](<../graphics/gtypes.c.md#grect_align>)
    - [`graphics_fill_oval`](<../graphics/graphics_circle.c.md#graphics_fill_oval>)


---
### action\_button\_update\_proc<!-- {{#callable:action_button_update_proc}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_button.c#L49>)

Calls [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>) to render an action button on a specified layer with a black fill color.
- **Inputs**:
    - ``action_button_layer``: A pointer to a `Layer` object where the action button will be drawn.
    - ``ctx``: A pointer to a `GContext` object used for drawing operations.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Calls the [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>) function with `ctx`, `action_button_layer`, and `GColorBlack` as arguments.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`action_button_draw`](<#action_button_draw>)
